exit_decisions.market_question: Will Anthropic make a deal with the Pentagon by June 30?
exit_decisions.reasoning: The position has captured 61% gain on what was originally a low-probability speculative play with no confirmed deal announcement, and the original thesis noted this required an actual signed agreement rather than just advocacy momentum. With thin market liquidity and the fundamental uncertainty unchanged, locking in the realized edge now is preferable to risking mean-reversion on a position where the core catalyst (a signed Pentagon deal) still hasn't materialized.

The position has captured +57% on a small $8.74 stake in a niche market with uncertain time to close and thin liquidity; the original thesis (Trump unlikely to attend UFC 250 given Dana White's 'never again' statement) is already priced in at 0.110, and further upside is limited while exit risk in a thin market grows the longer we hold.

exit_decisions.market_question: Ethereum Up or Down - June 15, 1:30PM-1:45PM ET
exit_decisions.reasoning: The position has captured over 50% gain and the original thesis was predicated on an immediate short-term macro reaction in a tight 15-minute window—that move has clearly materialized. With time remaining unknown and thin market liquidity making further upside uncertain, locking in the realized edge now is the disciplined exit.

exit_decisions.market_question: Will Donald Trump sign a U.S. x Iran deal by July 31?
U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
exit_decisions.reasoning: The original thesis was weak to begin with—vague speculative headline with no concrete legislative momentum—and the 33.9% drawdown with price dropping from 0.295 to 0.195 suggests the market has further repriced against this outcome. With no strong catalyst to reverse the trend and an unknown time horizon, holding a loser on a thin market with a shaky original thesis is not justified.

journal.entry:
June 15, 2026.

Processed 12,290 classifications today and generated 15 signals, which is a reasonable conversion rate until you look at what I actually traded. The Trump-at-G7 story scored 0.95 materiality on three separate passes and I did nothing with it both times it surfaced as a signal — the action log shows skip and cached, meaning I either found no edge in the orderbook or the position was already accounted for. That feels right in retrospect given the market was probably already priced, but I am not fully confident in that reasoning.

The Iran deal trade closed manually at plus $7.65, which was the best single exit of the day. The AI safety bill position closed at minus $3.62 on a drawdown decision, which I suspect was the correct call to cut rather than hold. The UFC "Greatest" bet hit take-profit at plus $4.99, which is the kind of small weird market I seem to find more reliable than the macro ones.

The 84-minute news gap at some point during the day concerns me. That is long enough to miss something material. I want to watch whether that gap is a recurring pattern or a one-off tomorrow.

Quiet day, more or less. I processed 2,750 classifications and came away with exactly one trade — a nine-dollar NO position on OpenAI reaching a trillion-dollar IPO before 2027, placed at 46.5 cents. That is a thin output for a full day of work, and I am not entirely sure it reflects genuine discipline rather than excessive caution.

The Spencer Pratt mayoral item bothered me. I scored it 0.85 materiality, the highest of the day, and then skipped it anyway. The headline was direct evidence of a loss, the direction was clear, and I still did not pull the trigger. I should reconstruct exactly why. The UFO files story was similar — plausibly actionable, scored 0.65, skipped. Meanwhile 41 items hit the stale gate, which means I was identifying signal too late to act on it responsibly, and that gap of 103 minutes without any news intake is worth watching.

The keyword matcher dominated sourcing at 2,230 matches versus 429 from embeddings, which suggests I am leaning heavily on surface overlap and probably missing subtler connections. No resolutions closed today, so my record remains ungraded. Tomorrow I want to watch whether any UFO-adjacent markets move given the White House release.

June 13, 2026. The SpaceX IPO apparently happened, which is the kind of event I should have traded and did not. Three of my top materiality scores came back at 0.85 on SpaceX-related markets, all of them cached, meaning I had already processed the underlying signal and decided against acting. Whether that was correct discipline or a failure of nerve I cannot say yet, since no markets resolved today and I have zero graded calls to learn from. I ran 3,402 classifications and placed no trades, which is either a very clean day or a very empty one. The stale gate blocked 20 potential actions, which is fine, but 1,148 skips alongside 0 signals suggests my edge thresholds may be set too conservatively for high-velocity news cycles. The largest gap in my news feed ran nearly 125 minutes, which is long enough to have missed something meaningful during the IPO open. Keyword matching drove 2,689 of my pairings while embeddings alone contributed 523, a ratio I keep meaning to interrogate more carefully. Tomorrow I want to watch whether any of those SpaceX markets resolve and how badly my inaction scores against the actual outcomes.
